content:
Tabular ML moved from **ad hoc scripts**—fill NaN in place, scale the whole matrix, train a model—to **reusable transform objects** as libraries matured. Early **scikit-learn** (2010s) introduced **`Pipeline`** so cross-validation could not accidentally fit scalers on validation folds, and **`ColumnTransformer`** (sklearn 0.20, 2018) formalized **per-column-type** branches for mixed tables.

**pandas** (2008 onward) became the default in-memory cleaning layer: **deduplication**, **dtype coercion**, and **string normalization** happen in DataFrames, while learnable steps migrated into sklearn transformers that support **`fit` / `transform`** and **pickle/joblib** serialization.

MLOps practice in the 2020s treats the **fitted pipeline artifact** as the deployable unit—preprocessing plus model versioned together—reducing **train/serve skew** that plagued teams who saved only the estimator. Imputation and scaling **theory** stayed in statistics and preprocessing topics; pipeline orchestration became the engineering glue linking **load → clean → transform → model**.

timeline.label: sklearn Pipeline
timeline.description: Chains transformers and estimators with per-fold fit in cross-validation, making leakage from preprocessing visible and fixable.

timeline.label: ColumnTransformer
timeline.description: Applies parallel mini-pipelines to numeric and categorical subsets, then stacks outputs for a single estimator.

timeline.label: Artifact-first deployment
timeline.description: joblib-serialized full pipelines become the standard handoff from notebooks to batch and API scoring jobs.
